description: User can send a text message and receive a streaming response from Claude API
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173
Step 2: Verify the application loads with welcome screen
Step 3: Type 'Hello, how are you?' in the input field
Step 4: Press Enter or click the send button
Step 5: Verify the user message appears in chat immediately
Step 6: Verify typing indicator appears while waiting
Step 7: Verify streaming response begins appearing word by word
Step 8: Wait for response completion
Step 9: Verify complete response is displayed with proper formatting
Step 10: Verify conversation is saved to database

description: Streaming response can be stopped mid-generation
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173
Step 2: Start a new conversation
Step 3: Type a request for a long response (e.g., 'Write a 500 word essay about AI')
Step 4: Press Enter to send
Step 5: Verify streaming begins
Step 6: Click the stop button while response is streaming
Step 7: Verify streaming stops immediately
Step 8: Verify partial response is displayed
Step 9: Verify stop button changes back to send state
Step 10: Verify message is saved with partial content

description: Markdown rendering in assistant messages works correctly
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173
Step 2: Start a new conversation
Step 3: Send message: 'Explain the differences between let, const, and var in JavaScript'
Step 4: Wait for response completion
Step 5: Verify response contains properly rendered headings
Step 6: Verify response contains properly rendered bold text
Step 7: Verify response contains properly rendered italic text
Step 8: Verify response contains properly rendered bullet/numbered lists
Step 9: Verify response contains properly rendered blockquotes
Step 10: Verify response contains properly rendered inline code
Step 11: Verify response contains properly rendered code blocks with language badges
Step 12: Verify links are clickable and open in new tab

description: Code blocks have syntax highlighting and copy functionality
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173
Step 2: Start a new conversation
Step 3: Send message: 'Write a Python function to calculate fibonacci numbers'
Step 4: Wait for response with code block
Step 5: Verify code block has syntax highlighting
Step 6: Verify language badge shows 'python'
Step 7: Hover over code block to reveal copy button
Step 8: Click the copy button
Step 9: Verify 'Copied!' tooltip appears
Step 10: Paste clipboard content to verify code was copied
Step 11: Verify copied code matches displayed code exactly

description: Input textarea auto-resizes as user types
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173
Step 2: Verify initial textarea has default single-line height
Step 3: Type a short message (under 60 characters)
Step 4: Verify textarea remains single line
Step 5: Type until reaching end of line
Step 6: Verify textarea expands to second line
Step 7: Continue typing multiple lines
Step 8: Verify textarea continues to expand vertically
Step 9: Verify textarea does not expand beyond maximum visible height
Step 10: Delete content
Step 11: Verify textarea contracts back to minimum height

description: Shift+Enter inserts newline, Enter submits message
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173
Step 2: Click on input field to focus
Step 3: Type 'Line 1'
Step 4: Press Shift+Enter
Step 5: Verify cursor moves to new line
Step 6: Type 'Line 2'
Step 7: Press Enter without shift
Step 8: Verify message is sent
Step 9: Verify input field is cleared
Step 10: Verify new message appears in chat

description: Character count and token estimation displays correctly
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173
Step 2: Verify character count is visible near input
Step 3: Verify initial count shows 0 or empty
Step 4: Type a single character
Step 5: Verify character count updates to 1
Step 6: Type additional characters
Step 7: Verify character count increments correctly
Step 8: Verify token estimation is displayed
Step 9: Verify token count is approximately 1/4 of character count
Step 10: Clear input and verify count resets

description: Image upload and display in messages
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173
Step 2: Start a new conversation
Step 3: Click the attachment button (image icon)
Step 4: Select an image file from local filesystem
Step 5: Verify image thumbnail appears in input area
Step 6: Verify remove button is visible on thumbnail
Step 7: Click remove button
Step 8: Verify thumbnail is removed
Step 9: Re-attach the image
Step 10: Send message with image
Step 11: Verify message appears with image displayed inline
Step 12: Verify image is properly sized and visible
Step 13: Verify API call includes image data
Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173
Step 2: Start a new conversation
Step 3: Send message: 'My name is John'
Step 4: Wait for response
Step 5: Send message: 'What is my name?'
Step 6: Verify response correctly recalls 'John'
Step 7: Send message: 'Remember I like pizza'
Step 8: Wait for response
Step 9: Send message: 'What do I like?'
Step 10: Verify response correctly recalls pizza preference
Step 11: Send message: 'Create a variable for this in Python'
Step 12: Verify Claude references the context appropriately
Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173
Step 2: Start a new conversation
Step 3: Send message: 'Hello world'
Step 4: Wait for response
Step 5: Find edit button on user message
Step 6: Click edit button

Step 8: Modify the text to 'Hello world!'

Step 10: Verify original message is marked as edited
Step 11: Verify Claude responds to edited content
Step 12: Verify conversation history shows edited version
Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173

Step 10: Verify each conversation shows relative timestamp on hover

Step 10: Verify old code blocks update theme
Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173
Step 2: Open conversation settings
Step 3: Find temperature control
Step 4: Set temperature to 0 (deterministic)
Step 5: Send message twice: 'What is 1+1?'
Step 6: Verify both responses are identical
Step 7: Set temperature to 1.0 (balanced)
Step 8: Send same message twice
Step 9: Verify responses may differ slightly
Step 10: Set temperature to max value
Step 11: Send message and verify more creative output

description: Max tokens adjustment limits response length
steps: Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173
Step 2: Open conversation settings
Step 3: Find max tokens setting
Step 4: Set max tokens to 50
Step 5: Send message: 'Write a long story'
Step 6: Verify response is truncated around 50 tokens
Step 7: Set max tokens to 500
Step 8: Send same message
Step 9: Verify longer response
Step 10: Verify response doesn't exceed set limit
Step 1: Navigate to http://localhost:5173
Step 2: Open conversation settings

Step 4: Set top-p to 0.1

Step 7: Set top-p to 0.9
Step 8: Send same message
Step 9: Verify more varied vocabulary and topics
Step 10: Verify top-p setting is saved per conversation
